name: Airport
classification_notes: The specimen copy references Swiss Design and the International Typographic Style directly, and the typeface's name and aesthetic align with the mid-century European grotesque tradition.
summary: A seven-weight grotesque sans drawn from the Swiss International Typographic Style tradition, named with a nod to mid-century institutional signage.
description: Seven weights from Thin to Bold give Airport a practical range for editorial and identity work. The typeface draws its aesthetic from 1950s Swiss and German design movements, referencing the clean, systematic approach of the International Typographic Style. No italics or condensed cuts are offered, keeping the family focused and minimal.
foundry_blurb: Airport is a grotesque typeface offered in seven weights from Thin to Bold, with specimen copy referencing Swiss Design, the International Typographic Style, and mid-century design icons such as the Ant chair by Arne Jacobsen and lighting work by Ingo Maurer.

name: Airport X
classification_notes: The naming, specimen imagery and references to Swiss Design and International Typographic Style firmly place Airport X in the grotesque tradition, with a neutral, systematic character typical of mid-century European airport and wayfinding signage.
summary: A seven-weight grotesque drawn from the tradition of Swiss International Style and mid-century European wayfinding typography.
description: Airport X runs from Thin to Bold across seven weights, its specimen texts referencing the Swiss and German design movements of the 1950s and figures such as Arne Jacobsen and Ingo Maurer. The design sits in the clean, systematic register of standard grotesques associated with airport and public signage from that era. It suits editorial work and identity projects that call for neutral, rationalist letterforms without ornamentation.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry present Airport X as a grotesque typeface with seven weights from Thin to Bold, its specimen referencing the International Typographic Style that emerged from Switzerland and Germany in the 1950s alongside icons of mid-century modern design.

name: Ritual Mono
classification_notes: Ritual Mono is a monospaced typeface with a consistent fixed-width structure across all weights, placing it squarely in the mono category with no stylistic deviation toward display or slab treatments.
summary: A six-weight monospaced family with matching italics, suited to technical and editorial applications where fixed-width rhythm is required.
description: Ritual Mono spans six weights from Extralight to Bold, each with a corresponding italic, giving designers a full tonal range within a fixed-width structure. The specimen copy nods to typewriter history and interface contexts, suggesting a typeface comfortable in both print editorial and screen-based UI work. The naming and aesthetic point toward a considered, contemporary mono rather than a utilitarian coding face.
foundry_blurb: Ritual Mono is offered by Revolver Type Foundry across six weights from Extralight to Bold, each with an italic companion, presented as a versatile monospaced family for a range of typographic contexts.

name: Le Rosart Display
classification_notes: The naming and specimen copy reference French Baroque and 17th-century heritage, suggesting a high-contrast serif in the tradition of French Baroque letterforms, positioned specifically as a display cut.
summary: A five-weight display serif drawing on French Baroque letterform traditions, available with matching italics throughout.
description: Le Rosart Display is a high-contrast serif family with five weights from Light to Bold, each paired with an italic. The specimen copy references French Baroque architecture and 17th-century couture, placing the typeface firmly in a historical register suited to editorial headlines, luxury branding and cultural publishing. The Display designation signals that the optical sizing is tuned for large-scale use.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry present Le Rosart Display as a serif family referencing French Baroque heritage, offered in five weights from Light to Bold with matching italics, intended for display-scale typographic applications.

name: Ritual
classification_notes: The even stroke weight, clean letterforms and geometric construction visible in the specimen point clearly to a geometric sans-serif tradition.
summary: A geometric sans-serif from Revolver Type Foundry offered in nine weights, each with a matching italic.
description: Eighteen styles spanning nine weights from Thin to Black, each paired with an italic. The family reads as a clean, even-tempered geometric sans suited to editorial, branding and identity work across a range of weights. The full range from Thin to Black gives it flexibility across both text and display applications.
foundry_blurb: Ritual is a typeface from Revolver Type Foundry, available in nine weights from Thin to Black, each accompanied by an italic, providing a broad and versatile range of styles for a variety of typographic applications.

name: Le Rosart Text
classification_notes: The family's naming convention and weight range with italics across all cuts points to a traditional text serif designed for extended reading, with proportions and optical tuning suited to body copy.
summary: A text serif family from Revolver Type Foundry offered in five weights, each with a matching italic, aimed at extended reading use.
description: Five weights from Light to Bold, each paired with an italic, give the family reasonable flexibility for editorial and book work. The Text designation suggests optical tuning for smaller sizes rather than display use. No designer credit or release year is listed on the page.
foundry_blurb: Le Rosart Text is offered by Revolver Type Foundry as a text-weight serif family spanning Light through Bold with full italic companions, presented through interactive specimen samples covering a range of astronomy and science-themed passages.

name: Le Rosart Textura
classification_notes: The name explicitly includes 'Textura', indicating a blackletter style rooted in the medieval textura quadrata tradition, characterised by dense, angular letterforms.
summary: A two-variant textura blackletter from Revolver Type Foundry, split into two distinct stylistic subfamilies.
description: Released in two separate cuts, Le Rosart Textura A and B offer distinct approaches within the textura blackletter tradition. The angular, medieval character of the letterforms makes it suited to display and editorial contexts where historical weight is required. The split into two subfamilies suggests meaningful stylistic differences between the variants rather than simple weight progression.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry offers Le Rosart Textura in two versions, A and B, each available to test and purchase separately through their type specimen tool.

name: Newson
classification_notes: The even stroke widths, clean letterforms and wide range of weights from Extralight to Black suggest a geometric or neutral sans-serif construction typical of contemporary text and display families.
summary: A seven-weight sans-serif family from Revolver Type Foundry, spanning Extralight to Black with matching italics throughout.
description: Fourteen styles cover seven weights from Extralight to Black, each paired with an italic, giving the family good range for both text and display settings. The even construction and neutral character suggest a workhorse designed for editorial, branding or UI work where consistency across weights matters. No condensed or monospaced variants are offered.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry presents Newson as a sans-serif family available in seven weights from Extralight to Black, each accompanied by an italic, offered for licensing via their website.

name: Damien Text
classification_notes: Damien Text presents as a text-optimised serif with regular, medium, semibold and bold weights alongside matching italics, suggesting a design tuned for sustained reading rather than display use.
summary: A text serif from Revolver Type Foundry offered in four weights, each with a matching italic, suited to editorial and long-form print work.
description: Four weights from Regular to Bold, each paired with an italic, give Damien Text a practical range for editorial and book typography. The naming convention and weight range suggest a design built around sustained legibility rather than display impact. Little contextual information is provided by the foundry, but the specimen copy, drawn from astronomical writing, sets a clear bookish register.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry offer Damien Text in eight styles spanning Regular, Medium, Semibold and Bold with corresponding italics, presented through an interactive specimen with astronomical-themed sample text.

name: Damien Display
classification_notes: Named 'Display' and offered in a weight range typical of display-focused families; the page presents it exclusively as a display typeface, with no text or body cuts mentioned.
summary: A five-weight display serif from Revolver Type Foundry, pitched at headline and editorial applications.
description: Five weights from Light to Bold give Damien Display a useful range for titling and large-scale print work. The family appears to be a focused, single-width display offering with no italic or condensed cuts. It suits editorial contexts where a composed, upright display serif is preferred over something decorative or expressive.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry presents Damien Display as a display typeface available in five weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold and Bold, each offered as individual styles for purchase.

name: Utility
classification_notes: The typeface presents clean, upright forms across seven weights with no italics visible, consistent with a functional grotesque or neo-grotesque without expressive variation.
summary: A seven-weight grotesque sans from Revolver Type Foundry, built for functional, technical and editorial applications.
description: Utility spans seven weights from Thin to Black, offering a clean, upright grotesque suited to digital interfaces, editorial layouts and technical documentation. The naming and specimen copy lean into themes of productivity, engineering and systems, giving the family a deliberately functional character. No italics or condensed cuts are present, keeping the family focused and straightforward.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry present Utility as a grotesque sans available in seven weights from Thin to Black, with specimen text drawn from themes of computing, technology and productivity.

name: Dinamit
classification_notes: The page presents a clean, multi-weight sans-serif family with italics across a wide weight range, consistent with a contemporary grotesque or geometric sans classification.
summary: A seven-weight sans-serif family from Revolver Type Foundry, each weight accompanied by a matching italic.
description: Spanning Thin to Bold across seven weights, Dinamit offers a full complement of upright and italic styles suited to editorial and branding work. The family follows a straightforward grotesque structure without condensed or extended cuts, keeping the offering focused. Little biographical or historical context is provided on the page, leaving the typeface to speak through its weight range alone.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry presents Dinamit as a multi-weight sans-serif family running from Thin through to Bold, with italic companions for every weight, available to test and purchase directly from the foundry.

name: Mondial Display
classification_notes: Marketed explicitly as a display typeface with a name suffix confirming its intended use; the weight range and italic variants suggest a refined, large-scale sans intended for headlines and editorial contexts.
summary: A display sans-serif from Revolver Type Foundry spanning five weights, each with a matching italic, suited to headline and editorial use.
description: Five weights from Extra Light to Bold, each paired with an italic, give the family enough range for varied typographic hierarchies at display sizes. The naming convention positions it as a companion or standalone display cut within Revolver's broader Mondial family. Clean and professionally executed, though the page offers limited detail on its specific design history or distinguishing characteristics.
foundry_blurb: Revolver Type Foundry offer Mondial Display in ten styles across five weights from Extra Light to Bold, with italics throughout, available to purchase by individual style or as a set.

name: Mondial Text
classification_notes: The typeface is positioned explicitly as a text family with optical sizing suited to body copy, offering book, medium and bold weights with matching italics, consistent with a contemporary text serif design.
summary: A text serif from Revolver Type Foundry offered in four weights from Regular to Bold, each with a matching italic.
description: A text-oriented serif family in four weights — Regular, Book, Medium and Bold — each accompanied by an italic. The weight range is well suited to body copy and editorial hierarchies, with the Book weight occupying the middle ground between Regular and Medium. No condensed or display variants are listed, suggesting the family is focused squarely on sustained reading at text sizes.
foundry_blurb: Mondial Text is available from Revolver Type Foundry in Regular, Book, Medium and Bold weights, each with an italic companion, available to purchase and test directly on the foundry's site.
